messages.content: In 30 words answer: 1.	Describe the basic concept of the 5G Non-Standalone (NSA) architecture. Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
5G Non-Standalone (NSA) architecture integrates 5G radio access with existing 4G core networks, allowing for faster speeds and lower latency while still relying on 4G for certain functions.

In 43 words answer: Explain the differences between the TDD and FDD 5G air interface. Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

TDD and FDD are two types of 5G air interfaces. TDD allows for flexible uplink/downlink ratio, while FDD has fixed uplink/downlink ratio. TDD uses a single frequency band, while FDD uses separate bands for uplink and downlink.
In 34 words answer: What is a split-bearer? Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

A split-bearer is a type of network architecture used in 5G that allows for separate handling of data that requires low latency from data that does not require low latency. This improves overall network performance.

In 34 words answer: 4.	Why are two UE transmitters required for a 5Goption 3 split-bearer connection? Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
Two UE transmitters are required for a 5G option 3 split-bearer connection because it allows for simultaneous communication over two separate channels, one with low latency and one without.

In 41 words answer: 4. Why are two UE transmitters required for a 5Goption 3 split-bearer connection? Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

Two UE transmitters are required for a 5G option 3 split-bearer connection because it allows for simultaneous communication over a low-latency channel and a non-low-latency channel, reducing the overall latency and enabling efficient use of network resources.
In 31 words answer:  What is a Bandwidth Part (BWP)? Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
A Bandwidth Part (BWP) is a portion of the total bandwidth that is used for a specific communication or transmission in a 5G network. It enables efficient use of network resources.
In 34 words answer: What is a CORESET? Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

A CORESET (Control Resource Set) is a set of physical resources in a 5G network that is reserved for control information, allowing for efficient and reliable transmission of essential system messages.

In 45 words answer: Explain how Dynamic Spectrum Sharing (DSS) can be used for spectrum refarming. Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

Dynamic Spectrum Sharing (DSS) allows 4G and 5G users to share the same frequency bands, allowing for more efficient use of resources and spectrum refarming. This reduces the cost and complexity of network deployment and maintenance while enabling greater network flexibility.

In 35 words answer: 8.	Why can the 4G and 5G part of a 5G NSA bearer be handed over independently from each other? Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
The 4G and 5G part of a 5G NSA bearer can be handed over independently because they operate on different planes. The 4G core network handles control plane, while the 5G RAN handles data plane.

In 30 words answer:9.	Explain the concept of the service oriented architecture for the 5G core network. Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

The service-oriented architecture for the 5G core network is designed around multiple, independent services, each with its own interface and functionality. This allows for greater network flexibility and customization.

In 27 words answer: What is the difference between Registration and Session Management? Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
Registration is the initial process of connecting to a network, while session management deals with ongoing data transfer and ensures uninterrupted service.

In 31 words answer: 11.	What is the difference between the RRC-Idle and the RRC-Inactive state? Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.
RRC-Idle is the state when a UE is not connected to the network, while RRC-Inactive is a connected state with no data transfer taking place, but the connection is maintained.

In 50 words answer: 12.	(a) Explain the concept of 5G network slicing.  (b) Identify any benefits for high-speed Internet access. Using this reference, Sauter, M. (2021). From GSM to LTE-advanced pro and 5G an introduction to mobile networks and Mobile Broadband. Wiley.

(a) 5G network slicing enables the creation of virtual, independent networks within a physical network to meet specific service requirements. (b) Network slicing enables the provision of higher-speed Internet access with better quality of service. It also allows for tailored services and greater network efficiency.
